meaning: adj.营养不良的,量小的,死板的
examples: The economy was hidebound by public spending and private monopolies.
London doesn't seem as hidebound by tradition as some of the other European cities.
The more hidebound European universities must be wondering what on earth they have started.
Hidebound Red Sox fans have finally come to embrace Wally since his 1997 unveiling.
His lessons are applicable to any hidebound grad student or business executive: Rewrite the rules.
